data.title: Skillsoft 10-K: $512.7M Revenue, $(16.27) EPS on FY2026 results
data.description:
Skillsoft reported full-year revenue of $512.7 million for FY2026, a 3.5% decline year-over-year, and a net loss per share of $(16.27) for continuing operations driven by impairment and other non‑cash items. The company recorded a net loss of $(139.8) million, which includes a $31.7 million impairment charge and substantial non‑cash adjustments that contributed to an operating loss. Management noted cost reductions and resource reallocations during the year as part of efforts to stabilize margins and focus on higher‑margin offerings.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenues: $512.7 million, down 3.5% year‑over‑year (FY2026 vs FY2025)
  • Operating income (loss): $(89.5) million, reflecting operating loss driven by impairment and amortization
  • Net income (loss): $(139.8) million, includes a $31.7 million impairment charge and other non‑cash items
  • Net income (loss) per share: $(16.27), basic and diluted for continuing operations
  • Costs of revenues: $134.6 million (reported); implied discussion of margin impact from GK mix and consumer declines noted in filing

Business Highlights

  • Revenue trend and channels: Total net revenues declined 3.5% driven by weaker GK enrollments and lower consumer Learner Platform sales; GK instructor‑led revenues fell approximately 13% year‑over‑year.
  • Shift in channel mix: Continued shift toward subscription and enterprise SaaS offerings as instructor‑led channels contract.
  • Product and brand momentum: TDS enterprise SaaS remained stable with slight growth in enterprise subscriptions while consumer demand softened.
  • Operational efficiency actions: Company implemented cost reductions through facility consolidation and a July 2024 resource reallocation plan that lowered G&A, sales & marketing, and development expenses.
  • Strategic initiatives: Ongoing review of strategic alternatives for GK, continued technology investments, and focus on reallocating capital to higher‑margin offerings to improve long‑term profitability.

Original SEC Filing: Skillsoft Corp. [ SKIL ] - 10-K - Apr. 07, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Aehr Test Systems Q3 revenue misses estimates
data.description:
Overview

  • US semiconductor test solutions provider's fiscal Q3 revenue fell and missed analyst expectations

  • Adjusted net loss for fiscal Q3 was smaller than consensus estimates

  • Company reported strong bookings and high book-to-bill ratio

Outlook

  • Aehr expects fiscal 2026 revenue at the high end of $45 mln to $50 mln range

  • Company reiterates H2 fiscal 2026 revenue guidance of $25 mln to $30 mln

  • Aehr expects H2 fiscal 2026 bookings at high end of $60 mln to $80 mln range

Result Drivers

  • STRONG BOOKINGS - Co reported $37.2 mln in bookings and a book-to-bill ratio over 3.5x, citing robust demand across wafer-level and package-level burn-in applications

  • AI PROCESSOR DEMAND - Increased orders and customer engagement for WLBI and PLBI systems for AI processors, especially for data center applications

data.title: Defense Stocks Can't Get a Boost From Iran War Escalations. What Would Move Them. — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

All eyes are on Iran, with President Donald Trump threatening to destroy a "whole civilization" if his demands aren't met. Escalating hostilities in the Middle East won't give defense stocks a boost, though. There are a few reasons for that.

"Tuesday will be Power Plant Day, and Bridge Day," wrote the president in a Monday Truth Social post. He has promised to hit those targets if Iran fails to meet America's demands, including opening the Strait of Hormuz to shipping. "A whole civilization will die tonight, never to be brought back again," the President added on Tuesday. "47 years of extortion, corruption, and death, will finally end. God Bless the Great People of Iran!"

Hitting civilian infrastructure isn't allowed under international humanitarian law, but assets that are so-called dual use, that benefit a combatant's military and its civilians, can be targets in a war.

Defense stocks haven't received much of a boost from the Iran conflict despite U.S. efforts to increase production of missiles, interceptors, and autonomous drone technologies. Coming into Tuesday trading, the iShares Aerospace & Defense exchange-traded fund was down about 8% since fighting began, three percentage points worse than the S&P 500.

The moves make some sense. For starters, the stock market is forward-looking. It doesn't care about now; it cares about what happens one year from now. A year ago, investors started to realize that geopolitical tensions were rising and defense spending was on the move. That benefited stocks. Coming into Tuesday, the iShares ETF was up 66% over the past 12 months.

Gains have elevated defense stock valuations. Northrop Grumman and Lockheed Martin shares trade for about 24 times and 21 times earnings expected over the next 12 months, respectively, up from 17 times and 16 times a year ago. Investors have already baked in higher earnings growth.

At this point, sector stocks would need higher defense spending worldwide to keep rising. The good news, if it can be called good news, is that the outlook for spending remains strong. The U.S. is planning to increase inventories of key weapons, including missiles and drones. European nations are spending more as traditional alliances with the U.S. fray.

As for Iran, it would take something unexpected to boost sector shares again. Investors just aren't sure what victory will look like in Iran, whether it would be the removal of Iran's enriched uranium or the opening of the Strait of Hormuz, which would provide oil-price relief.

They also don't know exactly what the president has planned for Tuesday, or whether he will postpone the deadline as he has done before. Those are also reasons for recent stock weakness. Investors hate uncertainty.

"We believe it's highly unlikely that Iran agrees to U.S. terms," wrote Capital Alpha Partners analyst Byron Callan on Tuesday. "We see a higher probability of the conflict extending into fall 2026 than we do a collapse of the Islamic Republic [or] IRGC's will to fight in May 2026."

The iShares ETF fell 1% on Tuesday, giving back some of Monday's gains following the unveiling of Trump's $1.5 trillion defense budget proposal for fiscal year 2027.

The budget represents a 40%-plus increase over 2026 levels, a one-year increase not seen since the Korean War. There might be some concern that the relatively unpopular Iran conflict could reduce Congress's willingness to authorize higher spending. That concern might be overblown. Defense spending grew roughly 8% a year during the Vietnam War. It grew roughly 10% a year in the decade following that war's conclusion in 1975.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Kura Sushi USA's Q2 sales, profit beat estimates
data.description:
Overview

  • U.S. sushi restaurant operator's fiscal Q2 sales rose 23% yr/yr, beating analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EPS and adjusted net income for Q2 beat consensus, losses narrowed yr/yr

  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q2 beat analyst expectations, driven by improved labor leverage and sales

Outlook

  • Kura Sushi USA sees fiscal 2026 sales between $333 mln and $335 mln

  • Company expects to open 16 new restaurants in fiscal 2026, maintaining unit growth above 20%

  • Kura Sushi USA forecasts restaurant-level operating profit margin of 18.0% to 18.5% for fiscal 2026

Result Drivers

  • COMPARABLE SALES GROWTH - Comparable restaurant sales rose 8.6%, driven equally by higher customer traffic and increased average check (price/mix)

  • LABOR LEVERAGE - Labor and related costs as a percentage of sales declined, mainly due to operational efficiencies, pricing, and better sales leverage, partially offset by wage inflation

  • TARIFFS AND MENU PRICING - Food and beverage costs as a percentage of sales increased, mainly due to tariffs on imported ingredients, partially offset by menu price increases

data.title: U.S. Stocks Mixed as Wall Street Watches Trump's Hormuz Deadline — Market Talk
data.description: U.S. stocks stage a late-session recovery, as markets watch developments in the Middle East. President Trump's deadline for the reopening of Hormuz expires at 8 p.m. ET. The energy sector is among the few ones to gain in the S&P 500, while consumer is the biggest loser. Rising oil prices stoke inflation fears, bolstering bets on a hawkish Fed. Apple falls 2% on reports it faces setbacks on the development of a foldable iPhone. The S&P 500 rises 0.1%, to 6617, and the Nasdaq gains 0.1% to 22018, after spending most of the day in the red. The DJIA pares losses to close down 85 points, or 0.2%, at 46584. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

data.title: C.H. Robinson First Quarter 2026 Earnings Release and Conference Call Scheduled for Wednesday, April 29, 2026
data.description:
EDEN PRAIRIE, Minn.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--April 07, 2026--

C.H. Robinson Worldwide, Inc. ("C.H. Robinson") , announced today that it will issue its first quarter 2026 results after the market closes on Wednesday, April 29, 2026. The company will hold a conference call at 5:30 pm Eastern Time on the same day to discuss the quarterly results and answer live questions from the investment community.

Presentation slides and a simultaneous audio webcast of the conference call may be accessed at http://investor.chrobinson.com. To participate in the conference call by telephone, please call ten minutes early by dialing 877-269-7756.

An audio replay will be available at http://investor.chrobinson.com.

About C.H. Robinson

C.H. Robinson is the global leader in Lean AI supply chains. For more than a century, companies everywhere have looked to us to reimagine how goods move. Now, as we redefine what's next for the industry, that same drive fuels our commitment to Building Tomorrow's Supply Chains, Today(TM). Trusted by 75,000 customers and 450,000 contract carriers, we manage 37 million shipments annually, representing $23 billion in freight. We deliver tailored solutions across the world via truckload, less-than-truckload, ocean, air, and more. With our unique combination of human insight and Lean AI working as one, supply chains move faster, smarter, and more sustainably. As a responsible global citizen, we proudly contribute millions to the causes that matter most to our employees. For more information, visit us at chrobinson.com.

CHRW-IR

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260407968207/en/

CONTACT: FOR INVESTOR INQUIRIES, CONTACT:

Chuck Ives, Senior Director of Investor Relations

Email: chuck.ives@chrobinson.com

data.title: LEVI: Net revenues grew 14.1% and net income from continuing operations rose 26.3% year-over-year
data.description:
Net revenues rose 14.1% year-over-year to $1.74 billion, with net income from continuing operations up 26.3% to $177.1 million and diluted EPS at $0.45. The Dockers® business was fully divested, and a $200 million share repurchase was executed. Liquidity remains strong, with $1.6 billion available.

Original document: Levi Strauss & Co [LEVI] SEC 10-Q Quarterly Report — Apr. 7 2026

data.title: Essex Announces Release and Conference Call Dates for Its First Quarter 2026 Earnings
data.description:
SAN MATEO, Calif.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--April 07, 2026--

Essex Property Trust, Inc. announced today that it plans to release its first quarter 2026 earnings after the market closes on Tuesday, April 28, 2026. A conference call with senior management is scheduled for Wednesday, April 29, 2026 at 10:00 a.m. Pacific Time or 1:00 p.m. Eastern Time.

The first quarter conference call is open to everyone and can be accessed by:

Internet: Go to www.essex.com; click on Investors and the first quarter earnings webcast.

Phone: Dial toll-free, (877) 407-0784, or toll/international, (201) 689-8560. No passcode is necessary.

Replay: A rebroadcast of the live call will be available online for 30 days and digitally for 7 days. To access the replay online, go to www.essex.com and select Investors and the first quarter earnings webcast. To access the replay digitally, dial (844) 512-2921 using the Replay Pin Number — 13759660.

About Essex Property Trust, Inc.

Essex Property Trust, Inc., an S&P 500 company, is a fully integrated real estate investment trust ("REIT") that acquires, develops, redevelops, and manages multifamily residential properties in selected West Coast markets. Essex currently has ownership interests in 259 apartment communities comprising over 63,000 apartment homes with an additional property in active development. Additional information about the Company can be found on the Company's website at www.essex.com.

data.title: Levi Strauss Raises FY Guidance as Turnaround Bears Fruit
data.description:
(20:11 GMT) *Levi Strauss 1Q EPS 45c >LEVI
(20:11 GMT) *Levi Strauss 1Q Net $175.8M >LEVI
(20:11 GMT) *Levi Strauss 1Q Rev $1.74B >LEVI
(20:11 GMT) *Levi Strauss 1Q Adj EPS 42c >LEVI
(20:11 GMT) *Levi Strauss Raises FY26 View To Adj EPS $1.42-Adj EPS $1.48 >LEVI
(20:11 GMT) *Levi Strauss Raises FY26 Rev Growth to 5.5% to 6.5% >LEVI
(20:11 GMT) *Levi Strauss 1Q Direct-To-Consumer Rev Up 16% YoY>LEVI
(20:11 GMT) *Levi Strauss 1Q Wholesale Rev Up 12% YoY>LEVI
(20:11 GMT) Levi Strauss Raises FY Guidance as Turnaround Bears Fruit

By Kelly Cloonan

Levi Strauss & Co. bumped up its fiscal-year guidance following double-digit sales growth in its latest quarter as the apparel company sees its turnaround efforts paying off.

The San Francisco company also said Tuesday it has started a search for a successor to Chief Financial and Growth Officer Harmit Singh, who plans to transition to the role of special adviser before retiring.

Levi's now projects revenue growth of 5.5% to 6.5% for the fiscal year ending Nov. 29, compared with prior guidance of 5% to 6%. It now expects adjusted earnings per share of $1.42 to $1.48, compared with $1.40 to $1.46 previously. The forecast includes a 4 cent headwind from a higher tax rate and excludes Levi's Dockers business, which it has sold.

The updated outlook comes after Levi's logged higher profit and 14% revenue growth in its latest quarter, driven by growth across channels, regions and categories.

Levi's attributed the results to its turnaround strategy, which has included efforts to grow its direct-to-consumer business and sell more clothing items beyond the jeans it's known for. As part of the shift, the company has been expanding its offerings of tops, skirts and dresses and cutting down on its go-to-market timeline to chase trends.

Levi's said those efforts are allowing it to capture a larger market and grow at a faster and more consistent pace.

"Our strategic transformation is translating into higher returns and more profitable growth, enabling us to convert more of our strong revenue growth into bottom-line profit," Singh said, noting the company has also seen positive trends quarter to date.

Singh has led Levi's finances for more than a decade, and took on the added role of chief growth officer in 2023. He has also overseen the company's transformation efforts.

For the fiscal fourth quarter, Levi's posted a profit of $175.8 million, or 45 cents a share, compared with $135 million, or 34 cents a share, a year earlier.

Adjusted earnings per share were 42 cents, compared with estimates of 37 cents a share according to analysts polled by FactSet.

Sales came in at $1.74 billion, compared with analyst estimates of $1.65 billion. Organic sales, a figure that strips out acquisition, divestiture and currency effects, increased 9%.

Direct-to-consumer sales rose 16%, while wholesale sales gained 12%.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Formerra Announces Global Price Increase
data.description:
CLEVELAND, Ohio, Apr 8, 2026 - (ACN Newswire) - Formerra, a leading distributor of performance materials, today announced a global price increase of $0.02 / lb on all products. The increase addresses higher operating costs related to rising material costs, exacerbated by the conflict in the Middle East. The adjustment goes into effect on April 15, 2026, or as contracts otherwise allow. Formerra remains committed to maintaining ample inventory levels and the service excellence our valued customers deserve.

Customers should contact their Formerra representative for additional information.

About Formerra

Formerra is a preeminent distributor of engineered materials, connecting the world's leading polymer producers with thousands of OEMs and brand owners across healthcare, consumer, industrial, and mobility markets. Powered by technical and commercial expertise, it brings a distinctive combination of portfolio depth, supply chain strength, industry knowledge, service, leading e-commerce capabilities, and ingenuity. The experienced Formerra team helps customers across multiple industries to design, select, process, and develop products in new and better ways - driving improved performance, productivity, reliability, and sustainability. To learn more, visit www.formerra.com.

Media Contact

Jackie Morris

Formerra

Marketing Communications Manager

jackie.morris@formerra.com

+1 630-972-3144

SOURCE: Formerra

Source: Formerra

data.title: Key facts: Nutanix debuts Agentic AI, NKP Metal; Service Provider Central
data.description:
• Nutanix (NTNX) launched Agentic AI in early access, targeting H2 2026 GA. It offers virtualized AI infrastructure on Nutanix Cloud Platform with GPU-as-a-service, K8s, multitenancy and security.1
  • Nutanix (NTNX) launched NKP Metal: bare‑metal Kubernetes with integrated storage, VM/container parity, automation and lifecycle management. Early access to NKP PRO/ULT; GA planned H2 2026.2
  • Nutanix (NTNX) launches Service Provider Central with Nutanix Cloud Platform to offer multitenant IaaS, centralized governance, Prism access and automated workflows; availability slated for H2 2026.3
